observed_facts: Two runtime executions succeeded for the same workflow revision and logical correlation ID FORM-LEAD-1001.
The two inbound events occurred four seconds apart and share the same submission identity.
Each inbound delivery created one CRM lead and one notification.
All four external effects have a null idempotency key.
Final external state contains two CRM leads and two notifications for the same submission identity.
primary_hypothesis: The workflow or effect-dispatch layer treats each inbound delivery as a new lead event and does not enforce idempotency at the logical lead-submission level.
alternative_hypotheses: The upstream form provider repeated delivery after a transient condition and the workflow lacked replay detection.
The CRM and notification integrations do not enforce uniqueness on submission identity.
Runtime-report idempotency is distinct from business-effect idempotency.
The redacted payloads may contain meaningful differences that justify separate processing.
missing_evidence: Workflow material content is unavailable; only its digest is visible.
Raw lead payloads are redacted, so semantic differences cannot be ruled out.
The intended uniqueness scope of submission identity is not explicitly declared.
recommended_next_investigation: Inspect the workflow revision and external-effect dispatch path to determine whether CRM creation and notification are keyed by logical submission identity or per-delivery event identity.
